title: Aldercrest — Q2 Check-in
aiSummary: Phoebe and Sarah discussed Aldercrest's strong Q1 performance following their recent $400M pension close, and confirmed early access for Global Alts Miami. Sarah flagged a product request around automatic meeting outcome tagging in the analytics dashboard. Nina noted they are ready to start reviewing three allocator profiles Phoebe will share.

title: Aldercrest — Renewal Prep
aiSummary: Renewal discussion with James Harrington and Sarah Lin. Aldercrest is satisfied with platform ROI, citing three allocator meetings that progressed to due diligence in the past year. Pricing was discussed and they requested a proposal for the Enterprise tier to unlock analytics and priority event slots. No major objections raised.

title: Aldercrest — Global Alts Miami Prep
aiSummary: Pre-conference prep call covering Aldercrest's target allocator profile for Global Alts Miami 2026. Sarah and Ben have 14 confirmed meeting slots and requested three additional introductions to public pension CIOs. Phoebe confirmed Pinehurst and Larkspur are both attending and open to introductions.

title: Aldercrest — Post-Miami Debrief
aiSummary: Post-conference debrief. Aldercrest took 14 meetings at Global Alts Miami, three of which Sarah identified as strong pipeline prospects. Two allocators have already requested follow-up materials via the platform. Sarah noted that meeting scheduling through iConnections was significantly smoother than last year.

title: Aldercrest — Escalation: Analytics Access Issue
aiSummary: Sarah reported that their analytics dashboard was showing incorrect engagement scores for three allocator profiles over a two-week period. Phoebe acknowledged the issue, confirmed it was a platform bug affecting a subset of Premium clients, and escalated to the product team. A fix was deployed within 24 hours. Sarah appreciated the responsiveness.

title: Pinehurst — Q1 Review & Event Prep
aiSummary: Comprehensive quarterly review with Pinehurst's investment team. Robert confirmed they are increasing their alternatives target to 22% and are actively looking for direct lending and long/short equity exposure. Phoebe presented Brightwater Credit and Aldercrest as candidates. Diana and Allison requested detailed platform analytics on their engagement history.

title: Pinehurst — Renewal Discussion
aiSummary: Early renewal conversation. Robert expressed strong satisfaction with the platform, specifically citing the warm-introduction workflow and conference scheduling tool. Kevin flagged a need for better portfolio-level reporting across all manager meetings. No pricing concerns. Renewal confirmed for January.

title: Pinehurst — Onboarding New Analyst
aiSummary: Training session for Kevin Marsh, a new senior analyst joining Pinehurst's alternatives team. Covered the full platform workflow including manager search, profile filtering, meeting request flow, and post-meeting notes. Kevin asked excellent questions about API access for integrating platform data into their internal portfolio management system.

title: Vesper — Renewal Conversation
aiSummary: Renewal conversation with Vesper's IR and PM team. Oliver expressed satisfaction with the quality of introductions, noting that two family office meetings from Global Alts Switzerland progressed into active due diligence. Helena raised a question about pricing for their new emerging markets sleeve — whether it counts as a separate product under the subscription terms.

title: Vesper — EM Sleeve Launch Strategy
aiSummary: Strategy call for the launch of Vesper's new emerging markets rates and FX sleeve. Rajan outlined the strategy and target return profile. Phoebe identified six allocators on the platform with EM mandate appetite, including three family offices and two endowments. The team agreed to prioritize warm introductions before the Global Alts Miami conference.

title: Vesper — Q3 Check-in
aiSummary: Quarterly check-in. Helena reported that platform engagement was up, with 12 new allocator connections in Q3. She noted one introduction that did not go well due to a mismatch in liquidity expectations, and asked Phoebe to review the allocator profile filters for the macro category.

title: Mendoza — Global Alts Prep
aiSummary: Pre-conference preparation for Global Alts Miami. Claire confirmed they are looking at three private equity managers and two hedge funds for new allocations. Victor asked about scheduling priority — they have tight availability and want to ensure meetings are with decision-ready managers only. Phoebe agreed to flag meeting-request urgency levels.

title: Mendoza — New CIO Introduction
aiSummary: Introductory call with Claire Dupont, the newly appointed CIO of Mendoza Family Office. Claire is a former Goldman Sachs partner transitioning to the family office space. She outlined her investment philosophy — concentration in best ideas across PE, macro, and credit — and expressed interest in expanding the family office's manager relationships through iConnections.

title: Mendoza — Platform Feedback & Renewal Risk
aiSummary: Claire raised concerns about the quality of manager matches on the platform, noting that several recommendations did not match the family office's liquidity and ticket-size requirements. She also mentioned they are evaluating Albourne Partners as an alternative for manager sourcing. Phoebe committed to improving match quality and scheduling a dedicated filter-refinement session.

title: Larkspur — Annual Review
aiSummary: Comprehensive annual review with Larkspur's full investment team. Margaret confirmed the endowment met its CPI+5% target for the fiscal year, with alternatives as the primary driver. The team is actively expanding VC allocations and is interested in second meetings with Quartzlight Ventures and a second Palo Alto-based fund. Patrick requested improved reporting on total platform-facilitated meeting outcomes.

title: Larkspur — VC Manager Introductions
aiSummary: Naomi reviewed a shortlist of five VC managers surfaced through the platform. She narrowed to two based on vintage year alignment and team stability. Phoebe confirmed that Quartzlight Ventures is raising their Fund IV and would be an appropriate introduction. Naomi requested a warm intro call be scheduled within the next three weeks.

title: Larkspur — Mid-Year Check-in
aiSummary: Mid-year check-in covering platform usage and upcoming conference planning for Global Alts Switzerland. Margaret noted that the platform has been most useful for VC and emerging manager discovery, and slightly less useful for hedge fund introductions due to limited manager breadth in macro strategies. Phoebe noted she would work on expanding the macro manager roster.

title: Northwind — Q1 Business Review
aiSummary: Quarterly business review. Thomas highlighted strong deal flow from the platform in Q1, with four manager meetings progressing to full due diligence. Samantha requested expanded reporting dashboards and the ability to tag meetings by investment mandate. Marcus committed to escalating the reporting request to the product team.

title: Northwind — New Mandate Discussion
aiSummary: Daniel walked Marcus through a new $800M infrastructure mandate Northwind is building on behalf of a sovereign wealth fund client. The mandate targets mid-market infrastructure equity and debt co-investments. Marcus identified eight potential managers on the platform and agreed to facilitate introductions in tranches over the next 60 days.

title: Glenfield — Alternatives Expansion Brief
aiSummary: Strategy call on Glenfield's alternatives expansion from 8% to 14% of general account assets. Barbara and Michael outlined preference for investment-grade private credit, infrastructure, and low-net hedge funds. Regulatory capital constraints make liquidity a top priority. James agreed to curate a shortlist of ten managers across these categories.

title: Glenfield — Platform Training: New Team Members
aiSummary: Onboarding training for Lauren Park and David Elsworth, newly appointed to Glenfield's alternatives investment team. James walked through the full meeting request workflow, manager profile filtering, and the analytics dashboard. Both Lauren and David are analytical and asked detailed questions about data export capabilities.

title: Cobalt Bridge — Impact Strategy Discussion
aiSummary: Priya and Amara outlined Cobalt Bridge's updated investment policy, which now requires all new alternative manager relationships to have a documented impact thesis. Amara is building a climate tech VC sleeve and asked for warm introductions to funds with measurable emissions reduction targets. Olivia confirmed three Quartzlight-adjacent managers on the platform that match the criteria.

title: Cobalt Bridge — Standard Tier Review
aiSummary: Jason reviewed Cobalt Bridge's usage of the Standard tier and asked about the cost of upgrading to Premium. He noted that the Standard tier's limited meeting-request quota is a bottleneck now that the team is actively allocating to alternatives. Olivia presented a case for upgrading before their 2026 renewal and agreed to send a proposal.

title: Brightwater — Fund III Close Celebration
aiSummary: Celebration call following Brightwater's successful Fund III close at $2.2B, oversubscribed against a $1.8B target. Christine credited three significant LP commitments to introductions facilitated through iConnections, including one public pension and two insurance companies. Andrew noted they are beginning early planning for Fund IV and want to retain exclusive access to the platform.

title: Brightwater — Allocator Pipeline Review
aiSummary: Quarterly pipeline review. Andrew and Grace walked James through 18 active allocator relationships in various stages. Six originated from iConnections introductions. The team asked for better tracking of the full meeting-to-commitment journey within the platform, as they currently use a separate CRM for funnel tracking.

title: Stonemoor — Event Strategy for 2026
aiSummary: Annual event strategy planning session. Stonemoor is registering for Global Alts Miami and targeting allocators with Sun Belt real estate appetite. Karen has a specific list of insurance companies and public pensions they want to meet. Marcus Reid requested a one-pager on how the iConnections platform can help them pre-qualify meeting partners before the conference.

title: Stonemoor — Team Change: New IR Lead
aiSummary: William introduced Karen Whitmore as the new Head of Capital Formation, replacing the previous IR lead who departed for a family office role. Karen comes from Blackstone's LP Relations team. Marcus committed to a full platform onboarding session and updated the account's primary contact accordingly.

title: Halcyon — Q4 Review & Platform Deep Dive
aiSummary: Full quarterly review with Halcyon's CIO and IR team. Jonathan reported exceptional platform engagement in Q4, with the highest meeting-request conversion rate of any manager on the platform. Ingrid highlighted three institutional allocators in final due diligence, two of which came from iConnections introductions. They are renewing early at the Enterprise tier and want to explore API access for automated prospect syncing.

title: Halcyon — Roadshow Planning
aiSummary: Planning session for Halcyon's spring roadshow covering Boston, Chicago, and Singapore. Ingrid and Elena requested targeted allocator lists by geography and mandate type. Olivia identified 22 prospective allocators across the three cities. The team is particularly interested in connecting with insurance companies and pension funds looking for systematic, low-correlation strategies.

title: Quartzlight — Fund IV Fundraising Kickoff
aiSummary: Kickoff meeting for Quartzlight's Fund IV campaign on the iConnections platform. Taylor and Mei outlined a $600M target with first close by Q3 2026. They want to connect with university endowments and family offices with VC allocation appetite. Sofia identified Larkspur Endowment and Mendoza Family Office as priority introductions, pending permission from both parties.

title: Quartzlight — Platform Engagement Review
aiSummary: Mei reviewed platform usage stats with Sofia and expressed concern that their profile views had dropped 30% since November. Sofia investigated and found the drop correlated with a category filter change. The team agreed on profile updates to increase discoverability. Mei also flagged that their subscription renewal is coming up and they have budget constraints heading into the fundraise.
